sources.label: Eustat Bilbao rental reference
sources.note: Eustat's 2024 municipal row for Bilbao in the official rental-reference table lists EUR12.0 per sqm per month, EUR859 per month, and 74 sqm as the headline collective-housing values, with a wider reference band of EUR700-950 per month and 59-85 sqm in the same row.

sources.label: Bilbao City Hall official portal
sources.note: The Bilbao municipal portal publishes official housing, social services, and local administration information for residents and newcomers.

sources.label: Bizkaia Foral Diputacion housing services
sources.note: The Bizkaia Provincial Council publishes the official housing-support framework, rental subsidy schemes, and housing registry for Bizkaia residents.

sources.label: Basque Government Haurreskolak article
sources.note: The Basque Government says the Haurreskolak service remains free during the 2025/2026 school year, children can start once they are 16 weeks old, and the public 0-2 network offers 8,844 places across 238 schools in 180 municipalities.

sources.label: Euskadi Hezkuntza school enrollment portal
sources.note: The Basque education portal published by Eusko Jaurlaritza covers enrollment procedures, school directories, and public and concertado school information for the Basque Country.

sources.label: Osakidetza citizen services
sources.note: Osakidetza says patients can call their health centre in normal hours or use Emergencias de Osakidetza and Consejo Sanitario 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, while online services cover appointments, the health folder, and mobile access.

sources.label: Osakidetza centre and hospital finder
sources.note: Osakidetza keeps an advanced public finder by centre type, historic territory, and municipality, and the live catalogue includes Bilbao and Bizkaia health centres and hospitals plus an open-data download.

sources.label: Hospital Universitario Cruces official site
sources.note: Hospital Universitario Cruces is the main tertiary and university hospital serving Bilbao and Bizkaia, with paediatric, emergency, and specialist departments accessible through Osakidetza.

sources.label: CTB Barik fares 2026
sources.note: CTB's Barik fares valid from 21 February to 31 December 2026 list Bilbobus at EUR0.44, Metro service at EUR0.61, EUR0.71, and EUR0.78 for 1 to 3 zones, Bilbao tram at EUR0.44, and the Artxanda funicular at EUR0.42 with Creditrans in Barik.
sources.verifiedAt: Valid 21 Feb-31 Dec 2026 CTB fares page

sources.label: Euskadi family aid expansion FAQ
sources.note: The Basque Government's updated 27 February 2026 FAQ says the EUR200/month aid for children aged 0-3 is extended to 0-4 in 2026, all registered monoparental families with children under 4 can request that extension, and after age 4 each child can move to EUR100/month until the month before turning 7.

sources.label: Euskadi monthly family aid procedure
sources.note: The live procedure page says the 0-3 application must be filed within 3 months to receive the full aid and that an accepted 0-3 file carries EUR200/month per child.

sources.label: Seguridad Social prestaciones familiares Spain
sources.note: The Social Security publishes national family benefit entitlements applicable in Spain, including child birth payments and dependent-child allowances.

bilbao.childcare: Haurreskolak remains a free 0-2 early-education service in the 2025/2026 school year.
bilbao.mobility: CTB 2026 Barik fares: Bilbobus and Bilbao tram EUR0.44; Metro service EUR0.61-0.78 depending on zones.
bilbao.familyBenefits: Euskadi family aid in 2026: EUR200/month up to age 4, then EUR100/month to age 7 for registered monoparental families and third-or-later children.

sources.label: Primaria Municipiului Bucuresti official portal
sources.note: The Bucharest City Hall official portal publishes local services, housing registration, social services, and administrative information for residents.

sources.label: INS Romania housing and real estate statistics
sources.note: The National Institute of Statistics publishes housing prices, rental indices, and construction data for Bucharest and Romania.

sources.label: DGASPC Bucuresti child and social protection services
sources.note: DGASPC Bucuresti is the official Bucharest authority for child protection, family services, and social support programs for residents.

sources.label: Ministerul Educatiei school and cresa enrollment framework
sources.note: The Romanian Ministry of Education publishes the framework for crese (nurseries), gradinite (kindergartens), and school enrollment procedures.

sources.label: DSP Bucuresti public health authority
sources.note: The Bucharest Public Health Directorate oversees public health services, epidemiology, and health facility oversight for Bucharest residents.

sources.label: Spitalul Clinic de Urgenta Floreasca
sources.note: The main Bucharest emergency clinical hospital covering trauma, general surgery, and emergency medicine, with 24/7 admissions and paediatric emergency services.

sources.label: Ministerul Sanatatii public health portal
sources.note: The Romanian Ministry of Health publishes the framework for public health insurance (CNAS), health facility oversight, and patient rights for residents.

sources.label: TPBI fares page
sources.note: TPBI publishes the current integrated fares: a 7-day pass at 30 lei, a monthly surface-network pass at 80 lei, and a monthly integrated surface-plus-metro pass at 140 lei.

sources.label: Metrorex official fare information
sources.note: Metrorex publishes the official Bucharest metro fare table, including single-journey, multi-trip, and monthly pass prices.

sources.label: MMSS child allowance service page
sources.note: The live MMSS service page says the benefit applies to any child, filings go through the Primarie, AJPIS has a 15-day legal processing window, and payments are made monthly on the published schedule.

sources.label: Romania public services catalog
sources.note: The national public-services catalog confirms ANPIS as the provider and points applicants to the live online service entry.

sources.label: ANOFM employment and social protection agency
sources.note: ANOFM administers employment support programs, parental leave payments, and social protection benefits for Romanian workers and families.

bucharest.childcare: DGASPC Bucuresti is the official Bucharest authority for child protection, family services, and social support programs for residents.
bucharest.mobility: Monthly pass: 80 RON surface network / 140 RON integrated with Metrorex.
bucharest.familyBenefits: Official child-allowance service for any child; Primarie filing and monthly payment schedule published.

sources.label: Agenzia Entrate OMI Bologna residential market
sources.note: The official OMI observatory publishes biannual price and rental quotes for Bologna municipality zones, used as the official real estate market reference.

sources.label: Comune di Bologna housing and social services
sources.note: The Bologna municipal housing portal covers rental subsidies, social housing applications, and tenant support services.
sources.verifiedAt: Current 2026 Comune di Bologna housing portal

sources.label: Bologna municipal nido tariffs
sources.note: Comune di Bologna says direct and indirect municipal nidi share the same ISEE-based monthly tariffs, include the meal, and from the 2025/2026 school year apply a EUR20 reduction for full-time attendance to 17.30 versus 18.00 plus a further 10% reduction for standard exit by 16.30.

sources.label: Bologna conventioned nidi agevolations
sources.note: Comune di Bologna publishes the monthly agevolazione bands for conventioned nidi in 2026/2027: EUR833.30 up to ISEE 12,000, EUR798.30 for 12,000.01-20,000, EUR763.30 for 20,000.01-26,000, EUR688.30 for 26,000.01-30,000, EUR553.30 for 30,000.01-35,000, EUR428.30 for 35,000.01-45,000, EUR323.30 for 45,000.01-50,000, and EUR137 for new entrants above EUR50,000 or without ISEE.

sources.label: AUSL Bologna doctor and pediatrician choice
sources.note: AUSL Bologna says SSN enrollment gives access to a family doctor or trusted pediatrician; for children aged 0 to 6 the pediatrician is mandatory, and the choice is made within the territorial list of residence.

sources.label: AUSL Bologna service guide locations
sources.note: AUSL Bologna maintains a searchable service guide for locations, services, and CUP desks across the Bologna districts, with the regional green number 800 033033 published for citizen support.

sources.label: TPER urban fares page
sources.note: The public TPER fares page exposes Bologna urban subscriptions directly: monthly impersonal EUR39, annual impersonal EUR340, and annual personal EUR310, all valid in the urban area of Bologna with Trenitalia Tper stations inside the urban area.

sources.label: INPS family support overview
sources.note: INPS confirms that Assegno Unico and Bonus asilo nido are national programs rather than city-level Bologna benefits.

sources.label: INPS Assegno Unico e Universale per i figli
sources.note: INPS publishes the Assegno Unico rates: EUR57-281 per child per month depending on ISEE, with supplements for disabled children and large families.

bologna.childcare: Bologna municipal nido tariffs are ISEE-based, meal-inclusive, and discounted for shorter full-time schedules from 2025/2026.
bologna.mobility: Bologna urban subscriptions: monthly impersonal EUR39; annual impersonal EUR340; annual personal EUR310.
bologna.familyBenefits: INPS confirms that Assegno Unico and Bonus asilo nido are national programs rather than city-level Bologna benefits.

sources.label: Agenzia Entrate OMI Lugo 2H 2025 lookup
sources.note: In the official OMI lookup for Ravenna > Lugo > residential > 2H 2025, Abitazioni civili are quoted at EUR1,350-1,650/sqm and EUR5-6/sqm/month in B2/Centro Storico, EUR1,400-1,750/sqm and EUR5.1-6.4/sqm/month in B1 around the historic core, and EUR860-1,150/sqm with EUR3-4.1/sqm/month in E1/Frazione di Voltana.

sources.label: Bassa Romagna nursery fee rules
sources.note: The Unione della Bassa Romagna says Lugo nursery fees are approved each school year, remain ISEE-based, and include automatic reductions for economic situation and pluriutenza, with the 2026/2027 rules approved by Giunta resolution n. 191 of 11 December 2025.
sources.verifiedAt: Updated 5 Mar 2026 Bassa Romagna fee rules page

sources.label: Bassa Romagna nido support measure
sources.note: For the 2025/2026 school year, the Unione says Emilia-Romagna resident families with ISEE up to EUR26,000 receive a 55% reduction on the monthly nursery fee from December 2025, including Union-run and conventioned private nidi.
sources.verifiedAt: Updated 4 Mar 2026 Bassa Romagna nido support page

sources.label: AUSL Romagna
sources.note: AUSL Romagna identifies the regional public-health network and exposes services, structures and places of care, and choosing family doctors and pediatricians across Romagna.

sources.label: Ospedale Civile Lugo — AUSL Romagna hub
sources.note: The Lugo civic hospital is the local acute care facility serving Bassa Romagna, covering emergency, maternity, paediatric outpatients, and day hospital services.
sources.verifiedAt: Current 2026 AUSL Romagna Lugo hospital page

sources.label: Ministero della Salute SSN patient rights
sources.note: The Italian Ministry of Health publishes the LEA framework guaranteeing the minimum SSN healthcare entitlements for all residents.

sources.label: Start Romagna Trova Zona
sources.note: The live Ravenna-basin selector in Trova Zona lists Lugo as zone 740 in the official Start Romagna locality lookup.

sources.label: Start Romagna subscriptions
sources.note: Start Romagna publishes the Ravenna-basin monthly subscriptions table, with EUR32 for 1 zone and EUR43 for 2 zones.

sources.label: INPS family support overview
sources.note: National frame for Assegno Unico, which varies by ISEE and household composition rather than by city.

sources.label: INPS Assegno Unico e Universale per i figli
sources.note: INPS publishes the Assegno Unico rates: EUR57-281 per child per month depending on ISEE, with supplements for disabled children and large families.

lugo.childcare: Lugo nursery fees are officially set each school year by the Unione della Bassa Romagna and remain ISEE-based with family reductions.
lugo.mobility: Lugo is listed in zone 740 of the Ravenna basin.
lugo.familyBenefits: National frame for Assegno Unico, which varies by ISEE and household composition rather than by city.

sources.label: Comune di Milano housing and real estate portal
sources.note: The Milan municipality portal covers public housing applications, rental market guidance, social housing, and the ISEE-based housing-subsidy system.
sources.verifiedAt: Current 2026 Comune di Milano housing portal

sources.label: Agenzia Entrate OMI Milan real estate market
sources.note: The official OMI observatory publishes biannual price and rental quotes for all Milan municipality zones, used as the basis for tax assessments.

sources.label: Comune di Milano contributive quote 2025/2026 PDF
sources.note: The official Comune di Milano contributive-quote PDF dated 1 July 2025 sets the registration fee at EUR56.20. For resident nidi, the monthly fee bands are free up to EUR6,500, EUR111.30 for EUR6,500.01-EUR12,500.00, EUR250.60 for EUR12,500.01-EUR27,000.00, and EUR502.20 for Above EUR27,000.00 or without ISEE; the non-resident nido rate is EUR669.35/month. For sezioni primavera, the resident bands are free, EUR55.65, EUR125.30, and EUR251.10, with a non-resident rate of EUR334.68/month.
sources.verifiedAt: Comune di Milano PDF dated 1 Jul 2025 for educational year 2025/2026

sources.label: Comune di Milano nidi and sezioni primavera service
sources.note: Comune di Milano's service page, updated 12 May 2026, says the 2026/2027 admissions cycle for nidi and sezioni primavera closed on 25 February 2026 and points families to the official attachments, Geoschool, and the municipal 0-3 services pages for the current nursery system.
sources.verifiedAt: Updated 12 May 2026 Comune di Milano service page

sources.label: Comune di Milano 0-3 services overview
sources.note: The official 0-3 page says Milan nidi serve children from 3 months to 3 years, with sections for 3-12 months and 12-36 months, and links the 2025/2026 contributive-quote PDF plus the quota-revision and payment pages in the same municipal service cluster.
sources.verifiedAt: Updated 12 May 2026 Comune di Milano 0-3 page

sources.label: Comune di Milano nursery quota workflow
sources.note: The quota-revision page, updated 12 May 2026, lets families request a quota review from the start of the educational year until 30 April in defined hardship or error cases, and the assigned quota remains payable until the municipality confirms any change.
sources.verifiedAt: Updated 12 May 2026 Comune di Milano quota page

sources.label: Comune di Milano nursery payment attestations
sources.note: The payment page, updated 12 May 2026, explains telematic receipts and payment attestations for nidi and sezioni primavera and explicitly lists Bonus nido INPS among the accepted reasons for requesting a payment certificate.
sources.verifiedAt: Updated 12 May 2026 Comune di Milano payment page

sources.label: ATS Città Metropolitana di Milano
sources.note: ATS Milano is the metropolitan public health agency overseeing GP registration, vaccination, preventive health, and SSN access for Milan residents.

sources.label: ASST Fatebenefratelli Sacco hospital network
sources.note: ASST Fatebenefratelli Sacco operates major public hospitals in Milan, including paediatric, maternity, and emergency services under the SSN.

sources.label: Ministero della Salute SSN patient rights
sources.note: The Italian Ministry of Health publishes the LEA (Livelli Essenziali di Assistenza) framework guaranteeing the minimum SSN healthcare entitlements for all residents.

sources.label: ATM Milan subscriptions
sources.note: ATM lists the ordinary urban subscription for travel within the Municipality of Milan and the city sections of Trenord and S lines at EUR39 monthly or EUR330 annually.

sources.label: INPS family support overview
sources.note: INPS confirms that Assegno Unico and Bonus asilo nido are national programs rather than city-level Milan benefits.

sources.label: INPS Assegno Unico e Universale per i figli
sources.note: INPS publishes the Assegno Unico rates: EUR57-281 per child per month depending on ISEE, with supplements for disabled children and large families.

sources.note: The municipal nursery-services page states that the integrated public system serves children aged 3 to 36 months and links admissions, schedules, and fee/payment rules.

sources.note: Santa Maria Nuova is the main public hospital in Reggio Emilia, with emergency, paediatric, maternity, and specialist wards accessible through the SSN.

sources.note: The Italian Ministry of Health publishes the LEA framework guaranteeing the minimum SSN healthcare entitlements for all residents.

sources.label: SETA Reggio Emilia subscriptions

sources.label: Stadt Köln Wohnungsmarkt housing overview
sources.note: The Cologne municipal housing portal covers the local rental market, tenant rights, subsidised housing, and housing advisory services.

sources.label: Statistik Stadt Köln
sources.note: The Cologne statistics office publishes housing data, population statistics, and socioeconomic indicators for the city.

sources.note: Uniklinik Köln is the main university hospital serving Cologne, offering all major specialties including paediatrics, emergency medicine, and maternity.

sources.note: The Cologne public health office covers infectious disease, environmental health, mother-and-child health, and health advisory services for residents.

sources.note: Source-linked transport anchor used for the Cologne household budget.

sources.label: KVB Köln ticket prices
sources.note: KVB publishes the Cologne tram and bus fare grid, monthly subscriptions, and annual pass options for the city network.

sources.note: Federal family portal states the current Kindergeld rate is EUR259 per child per month.

sources.label: Hospital Universitari i Politècnic La Fe
sources.note: Hospital La Fe is the largest and most specialised hospital in the Valencian Community, providing emergency, paediatric, maternity, oncology, and complex specialist care.

sources.note: The Valencian Community health authority explains SIP (Tarjeta Sanitaria Individual) registration requirements for EU residents.

sources.note: EMT Valencia publishes the municipal bus fare table, single journey prices, and the integrated TÍTOL bonobús card details.

sources.note: AEAT confirms the deducción por maternidad of up to EUR1,200/year for working mothers with children under 3.

sources.label: Wohnberatung Wien housing advisory service
sources.note: Vienna's official housing advisory service provides free consultation on rental rights, Gemeindebau applications, and affordable housing options.

sources.label: Statistik Austria housing statistics
sources.note: Statistik Austria publishes the housing price index, rental market trends, and household dwelling data for Austrian cities.

sources.label: City of Vienna childcare
sources.note: The City of Vienna states that kindergarten is free until compulsory school age. The page does not expose a machine-readable 0-3 municipal tariff, so infant-care costs stay withheld in the strict snapshot.

sources.label: MA10 Wien Kindergarten portal
sources.note: MA10 Wien publishes the full kindergarten and Krabbelstuben enrollment information, including free kindergarten policy and Bildungsbonus for Viennese children.

sources.label: Wiener Gesundheitsverbund (WiGev) hospital network
sources.note: WiGev operates the Vienna public hospital network, including Krankenhaus Nord, AKH Wien, and all district hospitals covering emergency, paediatric, and specialist care.

sources.label: Stadt Wien Gesundheitsamt health services
sources.note: The City of Vienna health portal covers public health services, vaccination, mother-child health, and GP finder for Vienna residents.

sources.label: Wiener Linien Jahreskarte
sources.note: EUR461/year, about EUR38/month equivalent.

sources.label: Wiener Linien ticket pricing
sources.note: Wiener Linien publishes the full Vienna transit fare grid, including single tickets, day passes, weekly, monthly, and annual passes.

graz.childcare: The City of Graz publishes municipal kindergarten enrollment, fee structures, and childcare service information for Graz families.
graz.mobility: Graz Linien monthly ticket from EUR66.60; Graz-subsidized KlimaTicket Steiermark variants EUR299 for eligible residents.
graz.familyBenefits: Official Austrian family-allowance table for the 0-3 bracket plus Kinderabsetzbetrag.

sources.label: Behörde für Stadtentwicklung Hamburg housing
sources.note: Hamburg's urban development authority publishes the residential rental market report, social housing programs, and housing subsidy information.

sources.label: Statistisches Bundesamt housing price statistics
sources.note: Destatis publishes the German residential property price index and rental market statistics by city size and type.

sources.label: Hamburg Kita-Gutschein childcare financing
sources.note: The Hamburg Kita-Gutschein system provides income-based subsidised childcare places for children from age 1, with the city covering most fees for eligible families.

sources.label: BMFSFJ Kindertagesbetreuung federal framework
sources.note: The Federal Ministry for Families publishes the framework for Kita quality and subsidy access including the Kita-Qualitätsgesetz and KiTa-Navigator links.

sources.label: GKV-Spitzenverband member rights and enrollment
sources.note: The statutory health insurance federation explains enrollment rights, free Krankenkasse choice, and covered services for new residents and employees.

sources.label: Hamburger Luftmessnetz oversight
sources.note: Hamburg BUKEA says the Institute for Hygiene and Environment has collected air-quality data since 1984 through the Hamburger Luftmessnetz. The city says 12 fixed representative stations currently monitor Hamburg, including continuous PM10 and PM2.5 measurements, and publishes the data online.
sources.verifiedAt: Reviewed May 2026 Hamburg BUKEA air-monitoring page

sources.label: Hamburger Luftmessnetz current data
sources.note: The current-data portal exposes station-by-station readings, pollutant views, day values, meteorology, and short-term limit comparisons from the Hamburger Luftmessnetz.
sources.verifiedAt: Reviewed May 2026 Hamburger Luftmessnetz current-data portal

sources.label: Deutschlandticket
sources.note: Current nationwide EUR63 monthly pass used as the official transport anchor for German cities in this expansion wave.

sources.label: Familienportal Kindergeld FAQ
sources.note: Federal family portal states the current Kindergeld rate is EUR259 per child per month.

sources.note: ONS publishes the UK house price index with regional and local authority breakdowns updated monthly.

sources.label: GOV.UK free childcare if working
sources.note: GOV.UK states the scheme covers children aged 9 months to 4 years and applications can be made once a child is 23 weeks old.

sources.label: NHS register with a GP surgery
sources.note: The NHS says everyone in England can register with a GP surgery for free. The page says residents do not need ID, proof of address, or proof of immigration status to register.
sources.verifiedAt: Page last reviewed 28 Jul 2025 NHS guidance

sources.label: NHS Find a GP service
sources.note: NHS England provides the official GP finder for locating and registering with a local practice in England.

sources.label: First West of England bus fares and passes
sources.note: First West of England operates most Bristol bus routes and publishes adult fares, weekly and monthly multi-trip passes, and contactless capping.

sources.label: GOV.UK Child Benefit
sources.note: Current GOV.UK guidance for Child Benefit, including the weekly rate for the eldest or only child and high-income clawback rules.

sources.label: GOV.UK Tax-Free Childcare scheme
sources.note: GOV.UK explains that eligible working parents can receive up to GBP2,000 per year per child through the Tax-Free Childcare account top-up scheme.

sources.note: The Ministry of Health publishes the national essential healthcare levels (LEA) and SSN enrollment rights for all Italian residents.

sources.label: Busitalia Veneto Padua urban subscriptions
sources.note: Busitalia Veneto says TU1 subscriptions cover the Orange Zone inside the Comune di Padova. Ordinary subscriptions are EUR42.90 monthly or EUR418 annually for TU1, and EUR52.80 monthly or EUR528 annually for TU2.
sources.verifiedAt: Reviewed May 2026 Busitalia Veneto Padua subscriptions page

padova.childcare: The Padua municipal portal publishes nido enrollment, ISEE-based tariffs, and childcare service information for families.

sources.label: ASUIT Trentino online services
sources.note: ASUIT says TreC+ is the digital access point for booking visits and tests, changing your doctor, seeing reports, and accessing the electronic health record. The homepage also links GP and pediatrician search and emergency-department attendance.

sources.note: TPER (Trasporto Passeggeri Emilia-Romagna) is the main transit operator for Bologna and the metropolitan area, including San Lazzaro di Savena. The subscriptions page lists monthly and annual urban and area pass prices.

sources.label: AUSL Romagna — Distretto di Imola
sources.note: AUSL Romagna covers GP and paediatric registration, SSN enrollment, and the Ospedale Nuovo di Imola for residents of the Imola district. For specialist care, the Bologna IRCCS network is the primary referral system.

sources.label: SABus Imola — urban and provincial subscriptions
sources.note: Start Romagna (incorporating former SABus services) operates urban bus lines in Imola and provincial connections to Bologna and Faenza. Monthly subscription tariffs are available for the urban zone.

sources.label: AST Pesaro-Urbino — Distretto di Urbino
sources.note: The Azienda Sanitaria Territoriale Pesaro-Urbino covers GP registration, SSN enrollment, and access to Ospedale Santa Maria della Misericordia in Urbino for residents. Specialist care is referred to Pesaro or Ancona.

sources.label: Ministero della Salute SSN citizen guide
sources.note: The Ministry of Health publishes the national essential healthcare levels (LEA) and SSN enrollment rights for all Italian residents.

sources.label: Adriabus — Pesaro-Urbino provincial network
sources.note: Adriabus operates inter-city and provincial bus services connecting Urbino to Pesaro (45-50 min), Fano, and other Marche coastal towns. Urban Urbino services are limited; a regional monthly pass covers provincial routes.
